humanCost.iranDeathsNote: Iranian Ministry of Health reports 1,045+ confirmed deaths from airstrikes, military operations. HRANA (opposition group) estimates up to 7,000 when including indirect casualties and unreported deaths. Trump administration estimates 32,000. Figures remain heavily disputed. Source: CENTCOM, Iranian state media, HRANA, Reuters.
humanCost.usKIANote: 6 killed in Iranian ballistic missile strike on US operations center in Kuwait, March 2. 47 additional injured in same strike. Source: CENTCOM.
humanCost.israeliDeathsNote: Including 9 killed in ballistic missile strike on Beit Shemesh, March 1. 2 killed in IED attack in Gaza. Source: IDF spokesperson.
humanCost.gulfCasualtiesNote: Deaths in UAE, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait from Iranian retaliation. Mostly civilian. Source: Official statements, Reuters.
humanCost.iranWoundedNote: Iranian Ministry reports 6,000+ injured in airstrikes and military operations. 300+ children hospitalized. Source: Iranian state media, IRC reports.
humanCost.civilianDeathsNote: Iranian government claims 148+ killed in US-Israeli airstrike on girls' school in southern Iran (Mar 2). US disputes strike was intentional; target claimed to be IRGC facility. Independent verification pending. Source: Iranian state media, Airwars, disputed.

costModel._note: OpenClaw cost model. 'timeBased' items accumulate cost every day they are active. 'eventBased' items are one-time costs based on quantity × unit cost. OpenClaw updates quantities when new data appears in RSS feeds.

timeBased.source: LLM-verified (Official imagery and statements show USS Abraham Lincoln and USS Gerald R. Ford operating in support of Operation Epic Fury)
Estimate based on DOD cyber/intel budget

eventBased.source: Air Force, Lockheed Martin unit cost

projections: 3-week war scenario: Tens of billions in direct military costs
Penn Wharton estimate: $210B total economic impact if conflict extends beyond 30 days
Direct military cost (conservative): ~$65B for full campaign assuming 4-5 week timeline
1M+ affected by travel disruptions; 1,900 flights canceled across Middle East
Global oil market volatility; Brent crude spiked from $78/bbl to $92/bbl

homelandThreats.title: Austin Bar Shooting — Possible Iran-Motivated Attack
homelandThreats.text: On March 1, Ndiaga Diagne (53, naturalized US citizen from Senegal) opened fire on Buford's Backyard Beer Garden on W. 6th Street in Austin, TX, killing 3 and wounding 15 before being fatally shot by police. He wore a shirt with an Iranian flag design and a "Property of Allah" hoodie. Search warrant at his home revealed an Iranian flag and photos of Iranian regime leaders. FBI investigating as possible terrorism linked to US-Iran conflict. Authorities caution motive is not yet confirmed.
homelandThreats.sources: CBS News, CNN, Washington Post, TIME, Fox News

homelandThreats.title: FBI on High Alert — Nationwide Sleeper Cell Monitoring
homelandThreats.text: FBI Director Kash Patel mobilized counterterrorism and intelligence teams nationwide following the start of Operation Epic Fury. Joint Terrorism Task Forces are working to monitor and disrupt potential Iran-linked plots. Federal and local law enforcement have boosted security in major US cities. DHS issued an internal law enforcement bulletin warning of potential lone-wolf and cyberattacks — but has NOT issued a public NTAS (National Terrorism Advisory System) alert, drawing criticism from security experts.
homelandThreats.sources: Fox News, Fortune, HSToday, NPR, Newsmax

homelandThreats.title: Iran/Hezbollah Sleeper Cell Network — Documented Locations
homelandThreats.location: Houston TX, Los Angeles CA, Boston MA, Portland OR, Louisville KY
homelandThreats.text: US intelligence has documented Iranian and Hezbollah-linked sleeper cells across the United States over the past decade. These cells have been observed in Houston (explosive precursor stockpiling), Los Angeles (surveillance of Israeli/Jewish institutions), Boston (fundraising and logistics), Portland (recruitment), and Louisville (logistics). With Operation Epic Fury underway, all cells are under heightened surveillance by FBI Joint Terrorism Task Forces.
homelandThreats.sources: HSToday, JNS, Jerusalem Post, Fox News

homelandThreats.title: Iran Cyberattack Threat — 60+ Hacktivist Groups Active
homelandThreats.text: More than 60 hacktivist groups linked to Iran have been activated since the start of the conflict. Targets include US government systems, power utilities, financial networks, and healthcare infrastructure. Iran's own internet is down to 1-4% capacity following US strikes on communications infrastructure. US CISA is reportedly stretched thin. Palo Alto Unit 42 warns of likely escalation in cyberattacks against civilian targets.
homelandThreats.sources: Palo Alto Unit 42, Fortune, CNBC, CSIS, Nextgov

homelandThreats.title: State & Local Security Posture Elevated Across US
homelandThreats.text: State and local law enforcement agencies have elevated their security posture in response to the Iran conflict. Increased police presence at critical infrastructure, government buildings, and gathering places reported in New York, California, Texas, Florida, and other major states. Some states have activated National Guard support for key installations. State fusion centers are coordinating with the FBI and DHS to share intelligence on potential threats.

homelandThreats.title: No DHS NTAS Alert Issued — Security Experts Critical
homelandThreats.text: Despite the largest US military operation since the 2003 Iraq invasion, the Department of Homeland Security has NOT issued a public National Terrorism Advisory System (NTAS) alert. The last NTAS bulletin was issued on June 22, 2025. Security experts have criticized this decision, noting that internal law enforcement bulletins have been issued but the public remains uninformed about the elevated threat landscape.

theaterOps.name: Iran Mainland
theaterOps.description: ~3,700 combined US-Israeli strikes on nuclear facilities, IRGC bases, command centers, and conventional military infrastructure. Khamenei killed in initial strikes. Iranian missile launch capability reduced 86%.
theaterOps.locations: Natanz, Isfahan, Fordow, Tehran, Bushehr, Bandar Abbas

theaterOps.name: Persian Gulf / Strait of Hormuz
theaterOps.description: 2 carrier strike groups (USS Lincoln, USS Ford) plus 13 destroyers and supporting vessels. Iran launched 500+ ballistic missiles and 2,000+ drones in initial retaliation. Ongoing naval engagements and air defense operations.
theaterOps.locations: Strait of Hormuz, Arabian Sea, Persian Gulf

theaterOps.name: Lebanon / Hezbollah Front
theaterOps.description: Israeli strikes on Hezbollah military positions and infrastructure in southern Lebanon. Iranian-backed militant groups launching rocket barrages into northern Israel. Limited ground operations ongoing.
theaterOps.locations: South Lebanon, Northern Israel, Israeli DMZ

theaterOps.name: Yemen / Houthi Front
theaterOps.description: Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping resumed in solidarity with Iran. US Navy conducting air defense operations. Global shipping corridor disrupted; commercial vessels diverting around Cape of Good Hope.
theaterOps.locations: Red Sea, Bab el-Mandeb, Gulf of Aden, Southern Yemen

developments.text: Indian traveller stranded in Qatar takes long road to Riyadh -- Varun Krishnan was flying from Chennai to Barcelona when Iranian attacks stranded him in Doha. [Al Jazeera - Iran]

‘No endgame’: Why US Democrats say Iran war hearing has them worried -- Lawmakers say the White House has not clearly explained why the US entered the conflict, or how long it may last. [Al Jazeera - Iran]

Australia grants asylum to 2 more members of Iranian women’s football team -- Australia's home affairs minister says a total of seven members of the Iranian women's team have been granted asylum. [Al Jazeera - Iran]
Iran launches wave of heavy, multi-warhead missiles -- The Iranian military released footage of missile launches from what it described as a wave multi-warhead projectiles. [Al Jazeera - Iran]

Iran war live: Tehran says US, Israel have hit nearly 10,000 civilian sites -- Iran accuses the US and Israel of targeting civilians, saying more than 1,300 have been killed across the country. [Al Jazeera - Iran]

Democrats say White House offers no clarity on Iran war goals after 11 days -- Party members decry 'disturbing' lack of clarity following latest classified briefing on war justification and aims. [Al Jazeera - Iran]

US civil rights group documents ‘broad attack on Muslim life’ in 2025 -- CAIR releases annual report, warning discrimination is likely to continue as Iran war sparks a new wave of hate speech. [Al Jazeera - Iran]

Around 140 US service members wounded in Iran war, Pentagon says -- US Department of Defense says eight service members in critical condition as White House hails progress of assault. [Al Jazeera - Iran]
Unexploded missile extracted from house in western Iran -- Rescue workers carefully extracted an unexploded missile from a house in Kuhdasht City, following US-Israeli airstrikes. [Al Jazeera - Iran]
Lebanese priest killed by Israeli tank fire -- A Maronite Catholic priest has been killed by Israeli tank fire after it targeted a home in southern Lebanon. [Al Jazeera - Iran]

Aviation will feel effect for the rest of the year ‘even if conflict ends t -- Finlay Asher says the Iran war’s disruption to Middle East airspace could impact the aviation industry for months. [Al Jazeera - Iran]
Could the US-Israel war with Iran fuel global inflation? -- Oil prices are swinging as markets react to every twist in the conflict. [Al Jazeera - Iran]
Is control of Iran’s natural resources a factor in US strategy? -- Iran has vast oil as well as gas reserves and is a key supplier to China. [Al Jazeera - Iran]

German Chancellor concerned US-Israel have no exit plan for Iran war -- German Chancellor said there appears to be “clearly no joint plan” to bring the US-Israeli war on Iran to an end. [Al Jazeera - Iran]
Five stories you may have missed amid US-Israeli war on Iran -- Here are some news stories you might not have seen amid the United States-Israel war on Iran. [Al Jazeera - Iran]
‘America has no narrative for victory’ -- Andreas Krieg says the US is lacking a clear narrative for what victory in the war with Iran looks like. [Al Jazeera - Iran]
Moment ‘Hezbollah missile’ strikes Israeli radar station -- Israel has released video it says shows the moment a missile fired by Hezbollah struck a satellite and radar station. [Al Jazeera - Iran]
WHO warns of health risks from ‘black rain’ in Iran -- The World Health Organization warned that “black rain” caused by strikes on Iran’s oil facilities pose health risks. [Al Jazeera - Iran]

Global airlines hike ticket prices as Iran war sends costs soaring -- The war has sent oil prices surging, upending global travel and pushing airline ticket costs on some routes sky-high. [Al Jazeera - Iran]

‘Our hearts were shaking’: Tehran residents endure heavy Israel-US bombing -- The latest attacks come a day after bombing of fuel reserves created thick plumes of smoke that turned day into night. [Al Jazeera - Iran]

evidence.title: Enrichment Levels
evidence.text: Iran enriched uranium to 60% purity at Fordow underground facility. Rate: 1.1 kg/day as of May 2025. From 60% to 90% (weapons-grade) is a straightforward technical step — estimated weeks to months.

evidence.title: Stockpile Size
evidence.text: 408.6 kg of 60% enriched UF6 as of May 2025. Additionally: 274.5 kg at 20% enrichment, 5,508.8 kg at 5% enrichment. Far exceeds JCPOA limits (capped at 300 kg of 3.67%-enriched material).

evidence.title: Unexplained Uranium Particles
evidence.text: IAEA found traces of enriched uranium at undeclared sites in Iran. Iran has failed to provide a credible explanation. Could indicate undisclosed military nuclear research or weapons development activity.

evidence.title: Fordow Underground Facility
evidence.text: Built inside a mountain near Qom, originally covert until exposed in 2009. Designed to withstand aerial bombardment — its very construction indicates Iran anticipated potential strikes on its nuclear program.

evidence.title: Loss of IAEA Access
evidence.text: Since June 2025 Israeli strikes (Operation Midnight Hammer), the IAEA has been unable to conduct in-field verification. Current enrichment status, stockpile location, and facility damage are all unknown.

evidence.title: Breakout Timeline
evidence.text: Pre-strike estimate: weeks to a few months to produce enough weapons-grade uranium for one device. Depends on operational centrifuge capacity. Current capability unknown due to strike damage to Natanz and Fordow.

claims.claim: 85% of enrichment capacity and 95% IRGC ballistic missile capability destroyed
Nuclear program remains intact and will be rebuilt stronger
Natanz centrifuge halls completely destroyed; will take years to rebuild
Unable to verify any claims regarding damage or current enrichment status since June 2025
1,700+ deaths from US-Israeli strikes; majority are civilians including women and children
Strikes targeted military and nuclear facilities only; civilian casualties minimized through precision targeting

polls.sources: University of Maryland PIPA, Washington Post polls, TIME polling, Pew Research Center

regionalReactions.text: 73% of Israeli Jews support strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities. Widespread concern about Hezbollah retaliation from the north. Rally-around-flag effect among the public, but growing anxiety over regional escalation.

Gulf states had privately lobbied for US action against Iran's nuclear program pre-strike. Now expressing concern about escalation and blowback. Worried about oil market instability, Houthi attacks, and Red Sea shipping disruptions.

Mixed sentiment among Iranian diaspora communities. Some support regime change through military intervention. Others oppose foreign military action against Iran on principle, regardless of views on the Islamic Republic.

Divided response: France urges caution, Germany calls for restraint, UK broadly supports US action, Italy and Spain remain neutral. EU publicly aligned with US but privately concerned about escalation and refugee flows.

Significant anti-war protests in NYC, Washington DC, Los Angeles, Chicago. Large demonstrations in London, Berlin, Paris, Madrid. Rising global anti-war sentiment, particularly among younger demographics.
Both voted against ceasefire at UN Emergency Session. Russia condemns strikes as 'unprovoked aggression.' China calls for 'immediate restraint.' Neither has threatened direct military intervention.

diplomaticImpacts: UN Emergency Session (March 3): Ceasefire resolution tabled; Russia and China vetoed; no binding action taken
GCC Response: Unified call for de-escalation; stops short of condemning US-Israeli operations
Flight Cancellations: 1,900 flights canceled across Middle East in first 6 days of conflict
Travel Disruptions: 1,000,000+ people affected; tourists stranded; business travel frozen across region
Oil Markets: Brent crude spiked from $78/bbl to $92/bbl in first week; settled around $85/bbl
Shipping Insurance: Red Sea premiums increased 300-400%; merchants diverting around Cape of Good Hope
Iran-Iraq Border: Iraqi government condemns strikes; reports of Iraqi militias mobilizing against US bases in Anbar
Lebanon-Israel Border: Lebanese government demands international intervention; Hezbollah vows continued resistance
US Military Positioning: CENTCOM reinforces presence; 4 additional carrier strike groups authorized for deployment
Global Supply Chains: Semiconductor, pharmaceutical, and automotive supply chains showing disruption; long-term impacts uncertain

summary: As of March 10-11, 2026, confirmed reporting indicates the war has killed at least 1,255 people in Iran according to Iran’s deputy health minister, wounded more than 12,000 there, and left seven U.S. service members dead and about 140 wounded per the Pentagon. AP reported the death toll in Israel at 12, while Bahrain confirmed one fatality from Iranian attacks in the Gulf. Pentagon officials said U.S. forces have now struck more than 5,000 targets in Iran, with operations intensifying even as Iranian missile and drone attack rates have declined from their initial peak.

briefings.summary: As of March 10-11, 2026, confirmed reporting indicates the war has killed at least 1,255 people in Iran according to Iran’s deputy health minister, wounded more than 12,000 there, and left seven U.S. service members dead and about 140 wounded per the Pentagon. AP reported the death toll in Israel at 12, while Bahrain confirmed one fatality from Iranian attacks in the Gulf. Pentagon officials said U.S. forces have now struck more than 5,000 targets in Iran, with operations intensifying even as Iranian missile and drone attack rates have declined from their initial peak.

Confirmed reporting on March 10, 2026 indicates the Pentagon has acknowledged about 140 US service members wounded and 7 US military fatalities from Iranian attacks, while CENTCOM's latest publicly located fact sheet says US forces had struck over 3,000 targets in the first 7 days of Operation Epic Fury. Publicly accessible reporting located in this search also states that strikes on Iran had killed more than 1,250 other people in addition to Ali Khamenei, supporting an Iranian death total of at least 1,251. I could not confirm newer official totals for Israeli deaths, Iranian civilian-only deaths, Iranian wounded, Gulf-state deaths, or the requested weapons-deployment/use counts from named primary sources.

As of Monday, March 9, 2026, the clearest confirmed casualty count located in current reporting is from Iran’s deputy health minister Ali Jafarian, who told Al Jazeera that 1,255 people had been killed and more than 12,000 wounded in Iran after nine days of US-Israeli attacks, with officials saying most victims were civilians. Al Jazeera’s latest cross-border live tracker lists at least 13 people killed in Israel, 14 killed in Gulf states, and eight US soldiers killed, but the latest official CENTCOM statement found only confirms seven US service members killed in action as of March 8. For strike totals, the most specific official figure found was CENTCOM’s statement, cited by Al Jazeera, that US forces had struck more than 3,000 targets in Iran by March 7; no current official combined US-Israeli cumulative strike number was found in the searched sources.
